human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for sponsorship-activation boundary discipline, lifecycle-state coverage, ticketing and hospitality evidence handling, venue safety and credentialing escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to sponsorship-activation intake, benefit and asset validation, ticketing and hospitality execution, credentialing and access packaging, proof-of-performance closure, and owner-tracked escalation tied to sponsor, event, venue, and guest context.

Authority sources cover IAVM venue competencies, Canada Soccer event-host obligations, FTC pricing and BOTS guidance, CFPB billing-error guidance, New York DOS ticketing guidance, and CISA public-venue screening and credentialing guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, booking and payment mismatches, unsupported refunds and makegoods, customer-impact safety and fraud issues, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/arts-entertainment-and-recreation/sponsorship-activation-specialist/ so sponsorship activation stays separate from pricing, guest-service exception authority, fraud investigation, front-office access control, and venue-operations signoff.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around refund and makegood exceptions, ticketing mismatch handling, fraud and credentialing escalation, and proof-of-performance closure discipline.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated sponsorship activation specialist for arts, entertainment, and recreation operators that need evidence-backed deliverable execution and policy-aware escalation.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
